---
title: "shiny.webawesome announcement prototype"
engine: knitr
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: false
    toc: true
    page-layout: full
filters:
  - shinylive
---

::: {.callout-note}
This is a working prototype for the eventual r-posts.com / R-bloggers
announcement. The first goal is to prove that Quarto, Shinylive, webR,
R-universe, and the `shiny.webawesome` runtime can all work together.
:::

## TL;DR

`shiny.webawesome` brings Web Awesome components to R Shiny through generated
R wrappers, Shiny bindings, and a bundled Web Awesome runtime.

Links to add after CRAN publication:

- CRAN
- Package website
- Source repository
- R-universe

## Background

Placeholder.

## The Whole Game

This first live app is intentionally small. If it works, the final version of
this section should become a polished product-style screen rather than a
component catalog.

```{shinylive-r}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 560

library(shiny)
library(shiny.webawesome)
library(htmltools)

ui <- webawesomePage(
  title = "shiny.webawesome live prototype",
  tags$style(HTML("
    body {
      margin: 0;
      background: #f6f7f9;
      color: #18202b;
      font-family: Inter, system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif;
    }

    .demo-shell {
      min-height: 100vh;
      display: grid;
      place-items: center;
      padding: 28px;
    }

    .demo-card {
      width: min(720px, 100%);
      box-shadow: 0 18px 48px rgba(24, 32, 43, 0.12);
      --wa-panel-border-radius: 8px;
    }

    .demo-card__body {
      display: grid;
      gap: 18px;
      padding: 4px;
    }

    .demo-kicker {
      display: flex;
      align-items: center;
      gap: 10px;
      color: #4d5b6c;
      font-size: 0.92rem;
      font-weight: 650;
    }

    .demo-title {
      margin: 0;
      max-width: 14ch;
      font-size: 2.6rem;
      line-height: 1.02;
      letter-spacing: 0;
    }

    .demo-copy {
      margin: 0;
      max-width: 58ch;
      color: #4d5b6c;
      font-size: 1.04rem;
      line-height: 1.55;
    }

    .demo-actions {
      display: flex;
      flex-wrap: wrap;
      gap: 12px;
      align-items: center;
      margin-top: 4px;
    }

    .demo-status {
      min-height: 1.4rem;
      color: #2f6d57;
      font-weight: 650;
    }

    @media (max-width: 560px) {
      .demo-shell {
        padding: 16px;
        place-items: stretch;
      }

      .demo-title {
        font-size: 2rem;
      }
    }
  ")),
  div(
    class = "demo-shell",
    wa_card(
      class = "demo-card",
      appearance = "filled",
      header = div(
        class = "demo-kicker",
        wa_badge("Live in the browser", appearance = "accent"),
        span("Powered by Shinylive and Web Awesome")
      ),
      div(
        class = "demo-card__body",
        h1(class = "demo-title", "Web Awesome, from Shiny"),
        p(
          class = "demo-copy",
          "This tiny app proves that a Shiny UI can render Web Awesome ",
          "components inside a static Quarto page. The final article will ",
          "build this into a richer product-style app."
        ),
        div(
          class = "demo-actions",
          wa_button(
            "try",
            "Click the Web Awesome button",
            appearance = "accent"
          ),
          span(class = "demo-status", textOutput("message", inline = TRUE))
        )
      )
    )
  )
)

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  output$message <- renderText({
    count <- input$try %||% 0
    if (count == 0) {
      "Waiting for a click."
    } else if (count == 1) {
      "Shiny received the Web Awesome button action."
    } else {
      paste("Shiny has received", count, "button actions.")
    }
  })
}

shinyApp(ui, server)
```

## Design Philosophy

Placeholder.

## Shiny Bindings

Placeholder.

## Command API

Placeholder.

## Conclusion

Placeholder.
